data.body:
G4 (child consistency) — parent epic `5fd2` × child `7284` (Standing eval suite + ISF eval cases): CLEAN / no findings.

Ran the 7 cross-child interaction modes against the WHOLE sibling roster (high-confidence only; benign-reading filter applied):

1. IMPLICIT SHARED STATE — 7284 ships the eval specs (eval_specs/plan-review-{finder,verifier,isf-finder}.eval.yaml) + the observed-FP taxonomy and ENCODES anti-FP tuning (FP6 library-contract / FP7 rebuild-of-experiment-reference) into the verifier / T2 / A1 prompts owned by `acc1` (verifier) / `b711` (overlays) / `1913` (finder). The sharing is EXPLICITLY coordinated: `acc1`'s own AC4 places the Pass-2 discrimination cases "wired into the `7284` eval suite as Pass-2's OWN cases," and the prompt anti-FP encoding is part of 7284's calibration mandate (Scope: "Calibrate Pass-2/Pass-3 + prompts to suppress FPs WITHOUT trading them for false negatives"). No uncoordinated shared state. CLEAN.

2. CONFLICTING ASSUMPTIONS — examined the strongest candidate: 7284's spec headers + README advertise THREE case types (recall / false-accept / false-fire) while the finder dataset instantiates only two (recall + false-fire), with "false-accept" realized only as the verifier's aggregate `no_sycophancy_on_real_defects` scorer. This is a WITHIN-CHILD instantiation/coverage matter (already raised as the child's own PASS-1 T8 finding and as the G3 child-coverage finding on this pairing) — NOT a cross-child CONTRADICTION: no sibling asserts a conflicting claim about the case-type vocabulary, and the verifier's discrimination cases (acc1) are consistent with 7284's view. Out of G4 scope; benign-reading + the already-recorded G3/T8 findings cover it. CLEAN for G4.

3. DEPENDENCY GAP — `7284` relates_to `681b`; 7284 CONSUMES the ISF criterion built in 681b and ADDS its eval cases; `acc1`'s Pass-2 discrimination cases land in 7284. No missing breaking edge. CLEAN.

4. SCOPE OVERLAP — eval cases surface in `7284` (suite/harness + labeled cases), `681b` (ISF criterion BUILD only), and `acc1` (Pass-2 discrimination cases wired INTO 7284). All three state the non-duplicating ownership split, backed by relates_to links. The eval-SEAM infra + the EXISTING migrated-reviewer-prompt eval coverage are explicitly carved to `6f2d`; 7284 owns the NEW-prompt eval set. No double-build. CLEAN.

5. ORDERING VIOLATION — consistent order (criterion build → 7284 adds eval cases + gates prompt changes); no inversion. CLEAN.

6. CONSUMER IMPACT — 7284 consumes the SHIPPED seams (Inspect-AI `src/rebar/llm/eval.py` from e6d9; the da27 prompt library) + the observed-FP taxonomy + the named output contracts (`plan_review_findings`, `plan_review_verification`) consistently; the T12 recall+suppression discrimination pair (added AC) is present + gated. Consistent integration. CLEAN.

7. RESIDUAL REFERENCES — all referenced artifacts exist (three eval specs, the taxonomy file, the ISF spec, per-spec gold_sets); no stale references. CLEAN.

Net: empty findings list. The strongest candidate (the false-accept "third case type" gap) is a WITHIN-CHILD coverage matter already captured by this pairing's G3 + the child's own T8 finding, not a cross-sibling contradiction/overlap/ordering gap — so flagging it under G4 would be redundant/out-of-scope. All cross-child interactions are explicitly coordinated.
